fact: September 22 is almost always the exact day when day and night are perfectly equal all around the world - 12 hours of light, 12 hours of darkness!
Squirrels go absolutely bonkers around this time, frantically burying up to 25 nuts per day to prepare for winter - that's like hiding 175 snacks every week!
In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves start their color-changing magic show right around now, turning from green factories into red, orange, and yellow masterpieces.
September 22 marks the moment when the Sun crosses the celestial equator, making it shine directly over Earth's middle like a giant spotlight.
Monarch butterflies are in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south around this date - imagine flying from New York to California without stopping!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, September 22 is actually the first day of spring, when flowers start popping up everywhere.
The Moon looks extra bright and orange when it rises around this time of year in northern places - it's called the Harvest Moon because farmers used its light to work late.
Apple trees are bursting with ripe fruit right now in many places, and a single tree can produce enough apples to make 20 pies!
September 22 falls exactly in the middle of the zodiac sign Virgo, which means people born today are supposed to be super organized and detail-focused.
Bears in cold regions are entering hyperphagia around this time - a fancy word that means they eat up to 20,000 calories per day to get ready for hibernation!
September 22 is day 265 of the year, leaving exactly 100 days until New Year's Eve - it's like nature's countdown timer!
